title: [FIRRTL] Inconsistency when compiling two semantically equivalent fir code
body:
Here're two firrtl programs, `demo1.fir` and `demo2.fir`, expressing the idea that *if `en` is set, `z` is `x`; otherwise `z` is invalid*.

```firrtl
; demo1.fir
circuit MyModule :
    module MyModule :
        input x: UInt<1>
        input en: UInt<1>
        output z: UInt<1>
        wire y: UInt<1>
        y is invalid
        z <= mux(en, x, y)
```

```firrtl
; demo2.fir
circuit MyModule :
    module MyModule :
        input x: UInt<1>
        input en: UInt<1>
        output z: UInt<1>
        wire y: UInt<1>
        y is invalid
        when en:
            y <= x
        z <= y
```

When I compile `demo1.fir` into verilog by

```shell
firtool --verilog demo1.fir
```

I've got

```verilog
// Generated by CIRCT firtool-1.76.0-1-g086a19f12
module MyModule(
  input  x,
         en,
  output z
);

  assign z = en & x;
endmodule
```

, where the input `en` has an effect on the output `z`.

However, when I compile `demo2.fir` into verilog by

```shell
firtool --verilog demo2.fir
```

I've got

```verilog
// Generated by CIRCT firtool-1.76.0-1-g086a19f12
module MyModule(
  input  x,
         en,
  output z
);

  assign z = x;
endmodule
```

, where the input `en` has no effect on the output `z`.

I'm wondering if this is an inconsistency of the compiler. Thanks for your explanation.
